# 'branch restack' with spice.restack.method=merge and
# spice.restack.mergeAutoResolve=theirs auto-resolves a would-be conflict
# in favor of the new base, without interrupting the restack.

as 'Test <test@example.com>'
at '2024-05-27T18:24:42Z'

mkdir repo
cd repo
git init
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Initial commit'
gs repo init

# use the merge method, auto-resolving conflicts in favor of the base.
git config spice.restack.method merge
git config spice.restack.mergeAutoResolve theirs

# create a feature branch that modifies init.
cp $WORK/extra/init.feature.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
gs bc -m feature

# go back to main and modify the same lines in a way that would
# normally conflict with the feature branch.
gs trunk
cp $WORK/extra/init.new.txt init.txt
git add init.txt
git commit -m 'Change init'

gs up
stderr 'feature: needs to be restacked'

# restack: the merge completes without interruption because the
# conflict is auto-resolved.
gs branch restack
stderr 'feature: restacked on main'
! exists .git/MERGE_HEAD

# 'theirs' means the new base (trunk) wins the conflicting hunk.
cmp init.txt $WORK/extra/init.new.txt

# a merge commit was still recorded on the branch.
git graph --branches
cmp stdout $WORK/golden/graph.txt

-- repo/init.txt --
initial init

-- extra/init.new.txt --
changed init

-- extra/init.feature.txt --
feature's init

-- golden/graph.txt --
*   02844f9 (HEAD -> feature) Merge branch 'main' into feature
|\  
| * 57ab3b0 (main) Change init
* | 10ab8d9 feature
|/  
* d692027 Initial commit
